_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to create our own texts that experiment with the literary language features and devices we have encountered in our reading.

_source.w: We are learning to create our own texts that experiment with the literary language features and devices we have encountered in our reading.
y: Experimentation is how we develop our creative skills and find our own voice as writers.
Applying literary devices we have studied deepens our understanding of how they work.
Taking creative risks builds confidence and helps us grow as writers.
eg: We write a short story that uses a literary device we studied — like an extended metaphor or unreliable narrator.
We create a poem that experiments with sound, imagery and structure.
We write a narrative that uses dialogue, description and pacing techniques from texts we have analysed.
We experiment with different narrative perspectives and discuss their effects.
We craft a piece that deliberately creates layers of meaning through symbolism.
We share our creative work and discuss how our use of literary devices contributes to its effectiveness.
